footer.ctaHeading: Ready to discover Vannes the easy and enjoyable way?
footer.ctaBody: Board the Petit Train de Vannes for a guided tour combining history, medieval architecture and comfort. Discover the ramparts, the old town and the port of Vannes, effortlessly, with a clear audio commentary throughout the journey.
footer.tagline: The Petit Train de Vannes offers a guided tour through one of the most beautiful medieval towns in Brittany. Comfortably seated on board, visitors discover the ramparts, the old town and the port of Vannes in a relaxed and accessible way.

hero.label: Le Petit Train de Morbihan · Vannes
hero.description: The Petit Train de Vannes takes you to discover the medieval ramparts, old town and port in a guided circuit of 40 minutes. A simple, comfortable and accessible way to explore one of the most beautiful medieval cities in Brittany, effortlessly, with a clear audio commentary throughout the journey.
hero.tagline: Ideal for families, couples, seniors and visitors of all ages.
hero.googleBadge: The Petit Train de Vannes is rated 4.7 on Google, with over 1,300 reviews, making it one of the most appreciated tourist attractions in Vannes.
hero.rightCardHeading: Guided tour with audio commentary in 22 languages
hero.rightCardBody: French, English, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch, Russian, Chinese, Japanese, Swedish, Danish, Polish, Arabic, Croatian and Slovenian.
hero.rightImageAlt: The Petit Train de Vannes alongside the medieval ramparts
hero.noteImportanteBody: Booking is possible but not required. You can arrive directly at the departure point Place Gambetta and buy your tickets at the counter on site.
hero.noteImportanteBodyExtra: Not all seats are available to purchase online.

prices.heroDescription: Find all prices and booking information for the Petit Train de Vannes. This page covers individual and group rates, children's discounts, and how to book your guided tour of the ramparts of Vannes.
prices.reviewQuote: "A wonderful discovery of the ramparts of Vannes. I truly recommend this visit for the whole family."
prices.faqDescription: Find clear answers to the most common questions about prices, group discounts and how to book the Petit Train de Vannes.

faq.q1: What is the ticket price for the Petit Train de Vannes?
faq.a1: Prices depend on age and group size. For individual visitors, the standard adult ticket is 8,50 euros. Children aged 3 to 11 benefit from a reduced rate of 5 euros. Children under 3 are free.
faq.q2: Are there reduced prices for children?
faq.a2: Yes. Children aged 3 to 11 benefit from a reduced rate of 5€. Children under 4 travel free.
faq.q3: Do children need a ticket to board the Petit Train?
faq.a3: Children aged 4 and over need a ticket. Children under 4 board for free.
faq.a4: Yes. Group rates are available for groups of 20 or more passengers. Advance booking is recommended. Contact us directly for a quote.
faq.q5: What is included in the ticket price?
faq.a5: Each ticket includes the full guided tour of approximately 40 minutes, audio commentary in 22 languages, a child-friendly version, and a comfortable on-board experience.
faq.q6: Can tickets be purchased on the day?
faq.a6: Yes. Tickets can be purchased directly at the counter on site at the Place Gambetta departure point.
faq.a7: Yes. Online booking is available and recommended during busy periods.

routes.heroDescriptionP1: Discover the full route of the Petit Train de Vannes through the medieval ramparts, the old town and the port.
routes.heroDescriptionP2: A 40-minute guided circuit with audio commentary in 22 languages, combining the most beautiful sights of Vannes in a single comfortable journey from Place Gambetta.
routes.heroImageAlt: Route map of the Petit Train de Vannes — click to enlarge
routes.faqDescription: Find clear answers to the most common questions about the route, stops and duration of the Petit Train de Vannes.
routes.faqQ1: What is the route of the Petit Train de Vannes?
routes.faqA1: The Petit Train de Vannes departs from Place Gambetta and takes you to discover the medieval ramparts, the old town with its half-timbered houses, the port of Vannes and the Jardins de la Garenne. The circuit lasts approximately 40 minutes.
routes.faqQ2: How long is the route of the Petit Train de Vannes?
routes.faqA2: The circuit lasts approximately 40 minutes. Visitors discover the main sites of Vannes at a relaxed pace, with clear audio commentary throughout the journey.
routes.faqQ3: Where does the route start and end?
routes.faqA3: The route starts and ends at Place Gambetta, in the heart of Vannes, steps from the medieval ramparts. It is the only departure and arrival point.
routes.faqQ4: Does the Petit Train make stops along the route?
routes.faqA4: The circuit is continuous — there are no intermediate stops. Visitors board and alight only at Place Gambetta.
routes.faqQ5: Will we see the medieval ramparts of Vannes during the visit?
routes.faqA5: Yes. The route follows the famous medieval ramparts of Vannes, among the best preserved in Brittany. The audio commentary explains their history and architecture throughout the journey.
routes.faqQ6: Does the route include the old town and port of Vannes?
routes.faqA6: Yes. The circuit passes through the old town with its 17th-century half-timbered houses and alongside the port of Vannes. These sites are presented through the audio commentary as part of the guided tour.
routes.faqQ7: Are the Jardins de la Garenne included in the route?
routes.faqA7: Yes. The circuit passes near the Jardins de la Garenne, one of the most beautiful gardens in Vannes, with a remarkable view of the ramparts and medieval washhouses.
routes.faqQ8: Is the Petit Train suitable for families?
routes.faqA8: Yes. The Petit Train is ideal for families with children and any visitor who wants to discover Vannes without walking far.

practicalInfo.label: Information
practicalInfo.heading: Practical Information
practicalInfo.description: To help you plan your Petit Train visit in Vannes, here is the essential information to know before booking.

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: The tourist visit lasts approximately 40 minutes. This circuit allows you to discover Vannes at a relaxed pace, from the ramparts to the old town and the port, without feeling rushed.

departure.title: Departure
departure.description: The only departure point is Place Gambetta, in the heart of Vannes, steps from the medieval ramparts. The stop is clearly signposted and easily accessible from the town centre.

schedule.title: Timetable
schedule.description: Departures run from April to October 8, plus the All Saints' school holidays, every 40 minutes. The train operates every day including Sundays and public holidays during the season.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: The Petit Train is ideal for families, seniors and anyone who wants to discover Vannes comfortably, without walking far.

payment.title: Payment
payment.description: Accepted payment methods: cash, cheques, chèques-vacances ANCV. Credit card payment is available only for online bookings. Group rates are available, contact us for a quote.

cta.heading: More information
cta.description: Consult our complete guide for ticket prices, payment methods and tips to make the most of your visit to the ramparts of Vannes.

prices.label: Prices
prices.heading: An affordable adventure for the whole family
prices.description: From solo explorers to large families, find the ideal rate for your visit to the ramparts of Vannes.

individual.title: Individual Tickets
individual.note: For individuals: buy your tickets directly at the counter on site at Place Gambetta. Free: children under 3 years old.

earlyBird.title: First morning departures
earlyBird.note: Reduced rate: applicable to first morning departures only. Tickets available at the counter on site on site.

group.title: Group Booking
group.text: For group rates, contact us directly:
group.note: For groups: reduced rate for groups of 20 or more passengers. Advance booking is recommended.

beforeYouBook.heading: Before you book
beforeYouBook.description: Before confirming your booking, take a moment to review the practical information about the Petit Train de Vannes visit. These points will help you prepare your visit and make the most of the ramparts circuit.
beforeYouBook.imageAlt: The ramparts of Vannes seen from the Petit Train
beforeYouBook.bullet1: Please arrive at least 10 minutes before departure
beforeYouBook.bullet2: Online booking is possible but not required
beforeYouBook.bullet3: Departure and arrival are at Place Gambetta
beforeYouBook.bullet5: Audio commentary is available in 22 languages, including a children's version
beforeYouBook.bullet6: The Petit Train operates in all weather
beforeYouBook.bullet7: Departure is guaranteed with a minimum of a few paying adults on board

features.label: Route Overview
features.heading: A simple and enjoyable way to explore Vannes
features.p1: The Petit Train de Vannes invites you to discover the city at a relaxed pace, comfortably seated on board a tourist train designed for visitors who want to enjoy Vannes without effort.
features.p2: During the visit, the audio commentary provides clear and accessible information about the ramparts, the old town and the port of Vannes. This tourist train is ideal for visitors looking for an enjoyable introduction to this medieval city and its history.
features.imageAlt: View of the ramparts of Vannes from the Petit Train
features.f1Desc: Discover the most beautiful sights of Vannes comfortably seated on board our tourist train, designed for visitors who want to explore the medieval city without effort.
features.f2Title: Audio commentary in 22 languages — included in the ticket
features.f2Desc: Audio commentary is available in 22 languages, with hygiene headsets provided, included in the ticket price. A children's version is also available.
features.f3Desc: Discover the medieval ramparts, the old town with its half-timbered houses, the port of Vannes and the Jardins de la Garenne in a single picturesque 40-minute circuit.
features.f4Title: Suitable for families, seniors and groups
features.f4Desc: Our train welcomes all ages and group sizes — no long walks, no effort, just a comfortable and enjoyable experience for everyone.

groupBookingCta.description: If you are travelling in a group or wish to hire the Petit Train de Vannes privately for an event, seminar or school visit, contact us for a tailored quote.

locations.description: Extend your visit to Morbihan with our other tourist little trains. In Carnac and Quiberon, discover new landscapes, unique heritage and unforgettable moments.

carnac.cta: Discover Le Petit Train de Carnac

ourLocation.label: Our Location
ourLocation.heading: How to get to the Petit Train de Vannes
ourLocation.description: The Petit Train de Vannes departs from Place Gambetta, in the heart of the city, steps from the medieval ramparts. Easily accessible by car or public transport.
ourLocation.arrivalTipDescription: Please arrive at least 10 minutes before departure to purchase your tickets at the counter on site.
ourLocation.item1Description: The only departure and arrival point. Located in the heart of Vannes, steps from the medieval ramparts. The driver sells tickets on site.
ourLocation.item2Description: Follow signs to Vannes town centre and Place Gambetta. Car parks are available nearby.
ourLocation.item3Description: Vannes train station is approximately a 10-minute walk from Place Gambetta. Local buses also connect the two.

informationsIntro.label: Important Note
informationsIntro.heading: Online Booking
informationsIntro.description: Booking is possible but not required. You can arrive directly at Place Gambetta and purchase your tickets at the counter on site.

routesTimeline.stop1Bullet1: In the heart of the city, steps from the medieval ramparts
routesTimeline.stop1Bullet2: Easy access from the town centre and nearby car parks

faqsSection.q1: What is the Petit Train de Vannes?
faqsSection.a1: The Petit Train de Vannes is a guided tourist tour aboard a little train that takes you to discover the most beautiful sights of the city: the medieval ramparts, the old town, the port and the Jardins de la Garenne. The visit lasts approximately 40 minutes and is accompanied by audio commentary available in 22 languages.
faqsSection.q2: How long does the visit last?
faqsSection.a2: The guided visit lasts approximately 40 minutes. The little train departs from Place Gambetta and returns to the same departure point.
faqsSection.q3: Where does the Petit Train de Vannes depart from?
faqsSection.a3: The Petit Train departs from Place Gambetta, in the heart of the city, steps from the medieval ramparts. It is the only departure and arrival point.
faqsSection.q4: Does the Petit Train de Vannes operate every day?
faqsSection.a4: Yes. The Petit Train de Vannes operates every day from April to October 8, plus the All Saints' school holidays, including Sundays and public holidays.
faqsSection.q5: Does the tour operate in bad weather?
faqsSection.a5: Yes. The Petit Train operates in all weather. In the event of exceptional severe weather, the service may be suspended for passenger safety.
faqsSection.q6: What will I see during the visit?
faqsSection.a6: During the visit, you will discover the medieval ramparts of Vannes, the old town with its half-timbered houses, the port of Vannes, the Jardins de la Garenne and the finest panoramas of the medieval city.
faqsSection.q7: In what languages is the audio commentary available?
faqsSection.a7: The guided audio commentary is available in 22 languages: French, English, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch, Polish, Russian, Swedish, Danish, Chinese, Japanese, Arabic, Croatian and Slovenian. The audio commentary is included in the ticket price. A version specially designed for children is also available.
faqsSection.q8: Is the visit suitable for children?
faqsSection.a8: Yes. The Petit Train de Vannes is suitable for children and families. The children's audio commentary offers a fun and educational experience.
faqsSection.q9: How much does a ticket cost?
faqsSection.q9Pre: Prices vary depending on age and group size. Adults pay 8,50€ and children aged 3 to 11 pay 5€. Children under 3 are free. Full prices are available on the
faqsSection.q10: Do I need to book in advance?
faqsSection.q11: Can I buy tickets on the day?
faqsSection.a11: Yes. Tickets can be purchased directly at the counter on site at the Place Gambetta departure point.
faqsSection.q12: How early should I arrive before departure?
faqsSection.a12: It is advisable to arrive at least 10 minutes before departure to buy your tickets and board.
faqsSection.a13: Yes. Group bookings are available for groups of 20 or more. Advance booking is recommended.
faqsSection.q14: Can I hire the Petit Train privately for an event?
faqsSection.q14Pre: Yes. The Petit Train de Vannes can be hired privately for corporate events, associations, school groups and private occasions. A dedicated request form is available on the

informationsScheduleLabels.operatingPeriodBody: The Petit Train de Vannes operates 7 days a week from April to October 8, plus the All Saints' school holidays, including Sundays and public holidays.
informationsScheduleLabels.weatherBody: The Petit Train operates in all weather. In the event of exceptional severe weather, the service may be suspended for passenger safety.
informationsScheduleLabels.saturdayRule: Public holidays: same schedule as regular days. The Petit Train is open on all public holidays during the season.
informationsScheduleLabels.onlineBookingNote: Online booking: available from Place Gambetta. Departures every 40 minutes during the season.
informationsScheduleLabels.closuresBody: The Petit Train is closed from 13 to 21 October as well as from December to March inclusive.
informationsScheduleLabels.viewPlanImageAlt: Route map — Petit Train de Vannes
informationsScheduleLabels.departureLocation: In the heart of Vannes, steps from the ramparts

routesHeroDefaults.googleBadgeText: Le Petit Train de Vannes is rated over 4.7 on Google, with over 1,300 reviews, making it one of the most appreciated tourist attractions in Vannes.

careersHero.description: Our philosophy is simple: to offer visitors a warm welcome and a smooth tourist experience while discovering Vannes and its ramparts. At the Petit Train de Vannes, every team member plays an important role in creating a friendly and memorable visit for people of all ages from around the world.
careersHero.job1Description: Drive the Petit Train safely along the Vannes ramparts route and welcome visitors on board. This role involves driving the train, ensuring passenger comfort and contributing to a positive atmosphere throughout the visit.
careersHero.job1Requirement: Category D licence (public transport) required. Prior experience with tourist vehicles is a plus.
careersHero.job2Description: Welcome visitors, sell tickets and provide clear information about timetables, prices and the Vannes circuit. This role is ideal for people who enjoy public-facing work and working in a lively tourist environment.
careersHero.job2Requirement: Good communication skills and a friendly attitude are essential. Knowledge of foreign languages is appreciated.
careersHero.job3Description: Assist passengers during boarding at Place Gambetta and ensure smooth departures and arrivals.
careersHero.job3Requirement: A positive attitude, reliability and ease with visitors of all ages are essential.
careersHero.imageAlt: Working at the Petit Train de Vannes

careersInfo.panel1P1: We are looking for motivated and reliable people who enjoy working with the public and contributing to a quality tourist experience in Vannes.
careersInfo.panel1P2: You are comfortable with international visitors, available during the tourist season and able to work weekends and public holidays if needed.
careersInfo.panel2P1: Positions are mainly seasonal and based in Vannes. Training is provided before starting. Working hours depend on the season and visitor numbers.
careersInfo.panel2P2: You will work outdoors and as part of a small, friendly team focused on visitor satisfaction.
careersInfo.panel3P1: To apply, please fill in the application form and attach your CV. If your profile matches our needs, our team will contact you.
careersInfo.panel4Heading: Join the Petit Train de Vannes team
careersInfo.panel4P1: Apply now and take part in a local tourist experience that helps visitors discover Vannes simply and enjoyably.
careersInfo.imageAlt: Who we are looking for at the Petit Train de Vannes

privatisationForm.imageDescription: The Petit Train de Vannes can be hired privately for group visits, corporate events and special occasions. This flexible solution allows you to offer your guests a guided tour through Vannes, comfortable and original, tailored to your needs and programme.
privatisationForm.imageAlt: Private hire of the Petit Train de Vannes
